text.title: User Story Mapping Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding User Story Mapping. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Backbone activities are 5-10 verb phrases ('Create Project'), never nouns ('Project').
rule.rationale: Backbone activities are 5-10 verb phrases ('Create Project'), never nouns ('Project').

rule.statement: Within each activity column, tasks are ordered top-to-bottom by priority; cap at 6 per column.
rule.rationale: Within each activity column, tasks are ordered top-to-bottom by priority; cap at 6 per column.

rule.statement: ≥1 error path or empty-state task per activity — not just happy path.
rule.rationale: ≥1 error path or empty-state task per activity — not just happy path.

rule.statement: Walking skeleton = exactly one task per activity, end-to-end completeable.
rule.rationale: Walking skeleton = exactly one task per activity, end-to-end completeable.

rule.statement: Each release slice spans the full backbone; partial-backbone releases ship broken journeys.
rule.rationale: Each release slice spans the full backbone; partial-backbone releases ship broken journeys.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-user-story-mapping.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
